title: Premium Homes unveils exclusive luxury duplex project in Lekki’s Osapa London
description: Premium Homes unveils exclusive luxury duplex project in Lekki’s Osapa London  Vanguard News
content: In an industry where trust, quality, and timely delivery remain major concerns for investors and homebuyers, Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Premium Homes and Property Consultants Limited, Ogbu Jideofor Ikenna, is redefining the standards of excellence in Nigeria’s real estate sector. Widely regarded as one of the industry’s most visionary leaders, Ikenna has built a reputation for delivering premium developments that combine luxury, functionality, and long-term investment value. Under his leadership, Premium Homes and Property Consultants Limited has grown from a property consultancy firm into a respected real estate brand known for integrity, innovation, and client satisfaction. The company’s latest project, an exclusive development of eight luxury five-bedroom fully detached duplexes in the prestigious Osapa London area of Lekki, Lagos, further reinforces its commitment to world-class housing delivery. Designed to meet the expectations of high-net-worth individuals, homeowners, and diaspora investors, each duplex features a private elevator, dedicated gym, private cinema, barbing room, swimming pool, spacious en-suite bedrooms, and ample parking space. The development also boasts premium architectural design and high-quality finishing aimed at delivering a sophisticated living experience. Industry observers say the project reflects Ikenna’s unwavering focus on quality and timely execution, a principle that has become synonymous with the Premium Homes brand. According to stakeholders, one of the company’s distinguishing strengths is its ability to deliver on promises, particularly in the off-plan segment where delays have become commonplace. By maintaining strict project timelines and prioritizing customer confidence, Premium Homes continues to attract investors seeking secure and high-yield real estate opportunities. Colleagues and clients alike describe Ogbu Jideofor Ikenna as a developer who consistently places the interests of buyers first, combining global best practices with a deep understanding of the Nigerian property market. With only eight units available, the Osapa London development is expected to attract significant interest from investors and luxury home seekers looking to secure prime real estate in one of Lagos’ fastest-growing residential destinations. As demand for premium housing continues to rise, industry analysts believe projects such as this demonstrate the growing capacity of indigenous developers to deliver world-class real estate products while maintaining international standards. For many observers, the success of Premium Homes and Property Consultants Limited under the leadership of Ogbu Jideofor Ikenna represents more than just business growth—it signals a renewed confidence in Nigeria’s real estate sector and a future where excellence, transparency, and innovation remain at the forefront of property development. Comments expressed here do not reflect the opinions of Vanguard newspapers or any emplo
summary: Premium Homes unveils an exclusive luxury duplex project in Lekki's Osapa London area, featuring 8 units with high-end amenities. This reinforces the company's commitment to quality and timely delivery, a key concern for investors and homebuyers in Nigeria's real estate sector. The project's focus on luxury and functionality suggests a growing demand for premium housing in Lagos.
trending_topics: luxury real estate,premium housing,Nigeria real estate

title: Why Monthly Car Rental in Abu Dhabi Is the Smart Choice for Business and Leisure Travelers
description: Why Monthly Car Rental in Abu Dhabi Is the Smart Choice for Business and Leisure Travelers  Tribune Online
content: Add as a preferred source Whether you are a corporate executive on a long‑term assignment, a business owner scouting opportunities, or a traveller planning an extended holiday, having dependable transport is essential. Abu Dhabi’s sprawling layout and world‑class infrastructure make a car almost indispensable. Yet buying a vehicle ties up capital, and daily rentals quickly become prohibitively expensive. That is why opting for a monthly car rental in Abu Dhabi has become the go‑to solution for thousands of professionals and visitors alike – offering the perfect balance of freedom, fixed costs, and hassle‑free mobility. Contents What Makes Monthly Rentals a Cost‑Effective Option? The Advantages of Choosing Yango Drive for Your Monthly Rental Seamless Online Booking Clear Pricing – No Hidden Fees Flexible Deposit Options 24/7 Customer Support Choosing the Right Vehicle for Your Needs Key Factors to Consider Before Booking a Monthly Rental Who Benefits Most from Monthly Car Rentals? Final Thoughts What Makes Monthly Rentals a Cost‑Effective Option? Monthly rental plans are designed to deliver substantial savings compared with daily or weekly hires. They also eliminate the financial weight of ownership. Here is why they make sound economic sense: Lower daily rates – The longer you rent, the less you pay per day. Monthly packages often slash the daily rate by 40‑60 %. No depreciation risk – You never absorb the vehicle’s value loss, which can be steep in the UAE’s dynamic used‑car market. Predictable monthly expenses – One all‑inclusive invoice covers the car, basic insurance, and scheduled maintenance, so you can budget with confidence. Zero upfront purchase costs – No down payment, no loan interest, and no registration fees. For businesses, this translates into improved cash flow and a simplified transport budget that can be adjusted month by month as project timelines evolve. The Advantages of Choosing Yango Drive for Your Monthly Rental While many rental companies operate in Abu Dhabi, Yango Drive stands out for its fully digital, transparent, and customer‑centric approach. The platform is particularly popular among corporate clients and expats because it removes the traditional red tape. Seamless Online Booking Everything from selecting a vehicle to uploading your driving licence and Emirates ID is done online. Approval is fast, and you can pick up your car at a convenient location or even have it delivered. Clear Pricing – No Hidden Fees Yango Drive displays the total monthly cost upfront, inclusive of basic insurance and service charges. You will not encounter surprise add‑ons at checkout. Flexible Deposit Options For many bookings, you can choose a plan with or without a security deposit – giving you extra control over your initial outlay. 24/7 Customer Support Should you face any issue – from a flat tyre to a question about traffic fines – support is available around the clock, ensuring you are never left stranded. These features make Yango D
summary: The article reports on the benefits of monthly car rentals in Abu Dhabi, highlighting cost-effectiveness, flexibility, and hassle-free mobility. This trend implies a growing demand for flexible and affordable transportation solutions in the UAE, which could have implications for Lagos' emerging middle class and the growth of its business ecosystem. Specifically, the 40-60% reduction in daily rates for monthly rentals could translate to similar savings for Lagos residents and businesses.

title: Fintiri Honoured With SCIRA 2026 Award For Excellence in Development
description: The Executive Governor of Adamawa State, His Excellency Rt. Hon. (Dr.) Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri, CON, has received yet another prestigious recognition for his outstanding performance in agriculture, infrastructure, and urban development at the States Competitiveness and Investment Readiness Awards (SCIRA) 2026, organised by the reputable BUSINESSDAY Newspaper in Abuja. The award, one of a series […]
content:
The Executive Governor of Adamawa State, His Excellency Rt. Hon. (Dr.) Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri, CON, has received yet another prestigious recognition for his outstanding performance in agriculture, infrastructure, and urban development at the States Competitiveness and Investment Readiness Awards (SCIRA) 2026, organised by the reputable BUSINESSDAY Newspaper in Abuja.

The award, one of a series of recognitions bestowed upon the Governor, is a testament to his transformational leadership, visionary governance, and people-oriented policies, which continue to drive sustainable development and improve the lives of the people of Adamawa State.

  • June 12: Governor Fintiri Seeks Greater Support for More Dividends of Democracy
  • Gov Fintiri directs appointees who defected to resign

The Honourable Commissioner for Information and Strategy, Barr. Leader Leneke, JP, received the award on behalf of Governor Fintiri and the good people of Adamawa State.
summary: The article reports on Adamawa State Governor Fintiri receiving a SCIRA 2026 award for excellence in development. This implies a positive sentiment towards state-led development initiatives, which may influence Lagos State's own development strategies. The award is a testament to Fintiri's transformational leadership and people-oriented policies, which could be a model for Lagos State's urban development.

title: Jim Ovia’s famous ‘real estate pays more than banking’ statement
description: Jim Ovia’s famous ‘real estate pays more than banking’ statement  Daily Trust
content: Recently, billionnaire businessman and founder of Zenith Bank Plc. Jim Ovia, , has announced plans to focus fully on real estate development following his exit as chairman of the bank, citing stronger profitability prospects in the sector than in banking. The 74-year-old billionaire businessman said he is deepening investments through Quantum Luxury Properties Ltd., where he is developing two high-end residential projects in Lagos, according to Bloomberg. The Lagos projects are a 26-floor Metropolitan Towers, with units starting from $1.85 million, and the 44-unit Quantum Luxury Towers, where apartments are priced from $2.8 million. Speaking from his office at Civic Towers in Lagos, Ovia said both developments are attracting strong demand and are already recording significant sales. “Real estate is more profitable than banking,” Ovia said, revealing a major shift in focus after more than three decades of building one of Africa’s most successful banking franchises. “I’m moving into real estate full-time,” he said. His move comes at a time when Nigeria’s property market, particularly in Lagos, is benefiting from rising demand for premium real estate amid persistent inflation, naira depreciation, and limited investment alternatives. The expansion into luxury housing also portrays broader trends in Nigeria’s property market with an estimated national housing deficit estimated at over 20 million units, demand remains strong across both affordable and premium segments. Join Daily Trust WhatsApp Community For Quick Access To News and Happenings Around You.
summary: Jim Ovia, billionaire founder of Zenith Bank, is shifting focus to real estate development, citing stronger profitability prospects. He's investing in two high-end projects in Lagos, with units priced from $1.85 million to $2.8 million. This trend suggests a growing demand for premium real estate in Lagos, driven by inflation, naira depreciation, and limited investment alternatives.

title: Real estate market to witness uneven growth amid pre-election uncertainties
description: Real estate market to witness uneven growth amid pre-election uncertainties  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Real estate market to witness uneven growth amid pre-election uncertainties By : Victor Gbonegun Date: 22 June 2026 4:00am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Abandoned Federal Government’s housing projects for civil servants in the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja. PHOTO: PHILIP OJISUA Abandoned Federal Government’s housing projects for civil servants in the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja. PHOTO: PHILIP OJISUA Nigeria’s real estate sector is expected to record uneven submarket growth in 2026, with infrastructure-led expansion and digital integration driving opportunities, even as pre-election uncertainties weigh on investment decisions. This was contained in the “2026 Nigeria Real Estate Market Outlook Report” by Panterra, which noted that previous election cycles showed a pattern of cautious investment behaviour, followed by selective activity as investors assessed market risks. The report stated that the 2011, 2015 and 2019 presidential election cycles reflected sensitivity to political transitions, with transaction activity typically slowing before gradually recovering. According to the study, the period leading to the 2023 elections witnessed increased liquidity in some real estate segments, although it remains unclear whether the surge was driven by genuine demand or speculative activity. “A strengthening naira, announced inflation reduction and relative foreign exchange stability are focus areas for the managers of Nigeria’s economy. There are also new considerations, including a more assertive approach by the United States towards emerging markets, which could moderate transaction volumes while encouraging more liquid, lower-risk opportunities,” the report said. The report noted that the real estate sector’s contribution to Nigeria’s economy remains resilient, accounting for about 5 to 6 per cent of GDP, but added that pre-election periods historically bring caution and reduced transaction volumes. On residential real estate, the report said affordable housing remains the biggest challenge, with low and middle-income segments suffering from persistent supply shortages. “Population growth will likely push more people into informal settlements. This undersupply, however, is absent in the luxury submarket,” the report stated. It added that short-term rental rates in Lagos and Abuja rose during the festive period due to increased demand, pushing some travellers towards hotel accommodation. The report noted that annual housing delivery remains below national requirements and may continue into 2026 as infrastructure spending receives priority attention. Although the Renewed Hope Cities and Estates Programme targets the delivery of more than 20,000 housing units annually, the report said the target was not achieved in 2025. “The residential market targeted at low- and middle-income earners represents the most significant unmet need,” it stated. It added that developers capable of overcoming financing challenges and lowering construction c
summary: The article reports that Nigeria's real estate market is expected to witness uneven growth in 2026 due to pre-election uncertainties, with infrastructure-led expansion and digital integration driving opportunities. This means that Lagos, being a key hub, may experience varying growth rates across different submarkets. The market is expected to contribute around 5-6% to Nigeria's GDP.
trending_topics: pre-election uncertainties, infrastructure-led expansion, digital integration, uneven growth

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The article's findings may influence investor sentiment and affect Mixta's ability to attract investors for its projects in Lagos.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The focus on infrastructure-led expansion and digital integration may create opportunities for Mixta to explore new technologies and partnerships in its projects, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The pre-election uncertainties may pose a risk to the execution of Mixta's projects, particularly if investors become cautious and reduce their investments in the real estate sector.

title: Developer raises concerns over bureaucracy, corruption in housing delivery
description: Developer raises concerns over bureaucracy, corruption in housing delivery  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Developer raises concerns over bureaucracy, corruption in housing delivery By : Eniola Daniel Date: 22 June 2026 4:00am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover An estate developer has identified bureaucracy and alleged corruption among some government officials as major obstacles limiting housing delivery and affordability in Nigeria, despite policies aimed at creating an enabling environment for private sector participation. The Chief Executive Officer of Harmony Gardens and Estate Development Limited, Saheed Audullahi, said while the Federal Government had introduced measures to support private developers and improve housing delivery, some officials in government ministries and agencies continued to create unnecessary bottlenecks. Audullahi alleged that some directors within government agencies had exploited loopholes in the system for personal gains, warning that such practices discourage developers from engaging in Public-Private Partnership (PPP) projects. Speaking with journalists in Lagos, he said Nigeria requires transparent systems that reward competence and patriotism rather than corruption. “We believe solving Nigeria’s housing crisis goes beyond building houses; it is about creating a sustainable system that makes home ownership accessible and affordable for everyday Nigerians,” he said. Audullahi said his firm was working towards democratising home ownership, stressing that housing should not remain a privilege for only the wealthy. He also expressed concern over the activities of fraudulent operators in the real estate sector, saying weak regulatory enforcement had allowed some individuals to disguise themselves as developers and use land banking schemes to defraud unsuspecting investors. “We currently lack proper regulatory enforcement, and that has created room for some people disguising themselves as developers to turn land banking into a Ponzi scheme. Related News Xenophobia: South Africa’s reputational erosion is not theoretical June 12: Adams, Odumakin, Obayori, others advocate restructuring to tackle insecurity Strengthening governance in Nigeria’s public sector “Their targets are mostly innocent Nigerians, especially those in the diaspora, who are lured with unrealistic return-on-investment promises. Some of these individuals market government-acquired land or properties that genuinely belong to other families with valid titles, all in a bid to defraud unsuspecting investors,” he said. He urged genuine developers to focus on value creation, infrastructure development, title perfection and community development rather than speculative practices. Audullahi commended the Federal Government’s housing initiatives under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Development, particularly the Renewed Hope agenda, describing support mechanisms through the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria and the Ministry of Finance Incorporated Real Estate Investment Fund (MOFI) as steps in the right direction. However, he said the government needed to inte
summary: An estate developer has raised concerns over bureaucracy and alleged corruption among government officials hindering housing delivery and affordability in Nigeria, despite policies supporting private sector participation. This implies a challenging business environment for developers in Lagos, potentially affecting the affordability of housing projects like Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe. The article suggests that Nigeria requires transparent systems to reward competence and patriotism rather than corruption.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The article's findings may affect Mixta's reputation and ability to attract investors, potentially impacting the company's future projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: None
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The risk of corruption and bureaucracy may hinder Mixta's ability to execute projects efficiently and effectively.

title: APWEN champions skills devt to bridge engineering gap
description:
The Association of Professional Women Engineers of Nigeria (APWEN) has reaffirmed its commitment to capacity development among female engineers as part of efforts to strengthen skills and close gaps in the engineering profession. The new Chairman of the Victoria Island

The post APWEN champions skills devt to bridge engineering gap appeared first on The Guardian Nigeria News - Nigeria and World News.
content:
The Association of Professional Women Engineers of Nigeria (APWEN) has reaffirmed its commitment to capacity development among female engineers as part of efforts to strengthen skills and close gaps in the engineering profession.

The new Chairman of the Victoria Island Chapter of APWEN, Nneamaka Ekechukwu, pledged during her inauguration and public lecture organised by the chapter in Lagos.

Ekechukwu urged female engineers to join the professional body to enhance their capacity, acquire relevant skills and improve their participation in the engineering sector.

She said APWEN was founded on the conviction that women working together could transform the engineering profession, industries and society.

According to her, members of the association have, over the years, broken barriers, challenged stereotypes and created opportunities for young female engineers.

“APWEN has been an advocate for advancing the professional development of practising female engineers, technologists and scientists, inspiring young girls to study science and engineering, and mentoring engineering students through seminars and conferences to ensure retention post-graduation,” she said.

Speaking on the theme, “A Global Call for Climate Action,” Ekechukwu said urgent and deliberate action was needed to address rising temperatures, flooding, waste pollution, energy poverty and other climate-related challenges.

She noted that climate action was no longer a future ambition but a present responsibility for engineers, stressing that engineering solutions were critical in addressing environmental threats.

“Engineers have been championing renewable energy solutions in our rural communities, building drainage systems. We are called to act. We must move beyond conversation to practical, measurable interventions,” she said.

In a presentation titled, “Climate Action Now: The Strategic Role of Women Engineers in Building a Sustainable Future,” a founding member of APWEN, Olufenmilayo Kadri, said governments, industries, professionals and communities across the world were confronted with the challenge of securing a liveable future for coming generations.

She said the answer lies in decisive climate action, adding that environmental challenges were no longer predictions but realities visible in rising temperatures, flooding, coastal erosion, pollution and biodiversity loss.

“Nowhere are these challenges more visible than in rapidly urbanising coastal cities such as Lagos. Yet it also illustrates the environmental pressures that accompany rapid growth. As professional engineers, we understand that development and environmental stewardship are not opposing goals. They must work together,” Kadri said.

She noted that future cities would not be measured only by the height of buildings or scale of investments, but by their sustainability, resilience and ability to protect people and the environment.

Kadri emphasised that engineers must remain at the centre of climate action, adding that solving global challenges requires diverse perspectives, inclusive leadership and innovative thinking.
summary: The article reports on the Association of Professional Women Engineers of Nigeria's efforts to bridge the engineering gap through skills development, with no specific numbers mentioned. This means for the Lagos market that there could be an increase in the number of skilled female engineers, potentially benefiting developers like Mixta Africa in terms of accessing a broader talent pool. The focus on climate action and sustainable solutions could also influence the design and implementation of future projects.
trending_topics: sustainable development, climate action, women in engineering

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Access to a broader talent pool of skilled engineers could enhance Mixta Africa's project execution capabilities
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Potential to incorporate more sustainable and climate-resilient design principles into future projects like Lakowe Crossings or Lagos New Town
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: None

title: Kwara: APC guber aspirants allege plot to force monarchs to back Speaker
description: Members of the Elders Caucus and 10 governorship aspirants under the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Kwara State have raised the alarm over an alleged plot to coarse traditional rulers in the state to back the purported emergence of the Speaker of the State House of Assembly, Hon. Yakubu Danladi Salihu as the party’s governorship […]
content:
Members of the Elders Caucus and 10 governorship aspirants under the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Kwara State have raised the alarm over an alleged plot to coarse traditional rulers in the state to back the purported emergence of the Speaker of the State House of Assembly, Hon. Yakubu Danladi Salihu as the party’s governorship candidate in the 2027 general elections.

The governorship aspirants and elders said they had uncovered plot by those hell-bent on foisting Salihu on the APC as its governorship candidate, to mobilise traditional rulers for a visit to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, to thank him for accepting and endorsing the Speaker as the party’s candidate for the 2027 general elections.

They also dismissed insinuations that the governorship aspirants had resolved to back the Director General of the National Orientation Agency (NOA), Mallam Lanre Issa-Onilu, as their preferred candidate, saying the DG never obtained any nomination form.

They raised the alarm during a meeting with the national leadership of the APC, held on Wednesday June 17 and Thursday 18 in Abuja.

The gubernatorial aspirants, who were in attendance at the meeting included Dr. Bashir Omolaja Bolarinwa; Senator Salihu Mustapha, Turakin Ilorin; Dr. Ibrahim Yahaya Oloriegbe; Alhaji Tajudeen Abdulkadir Audu, Makaman Lafiagi; Dr. Toyin Tajudeen Alabi; Rt. Hon. Mohammed Bio Omar; Dr. Muyideen Salako; Mohammed Dele Belgore, SAN; Ambassador Abdulfatai Yahya Seriki and Capt. Ahmad Mahmud.

The Chairman of the Kwara APC Elders Caucus, Chief James Bamisaye Ayeni, who led the delegation to the meeting, emphasised that no governorship primary election in the state produced the Speaker, adding that the party had at no time picked him as its consensus candidate.

The caucus said, “To the best of our knowledge, the party has not published the endorsement of any candidate in Kwara State. We would therefore like to caution the planners and masterminds of this desperate attempt, not to drag our highly respected Royal Fathers into an intraparty matter and affair. It is a settled fact that traditional rulers are fathers of all, irrespective of political inclination and they are insulated from partisan political calculations.

“Never in the history of our dear state has our Royal Fathers been dragged into affairs of any Political Party in particular and participating in politics in general. Therefore, dragging our Royal Fathers into the internal affairs of the APC is a sign of desperation, which is akin to decorating a Trojan Horse. It would be an unprecedented abuse of privileged positions by the planners and schemers of this mindless and inappropriate visit to Mr. President.

“We have it on record that despite the contentious circumstances that characterised the nomination of APC gubernatorial candidate in 2018, our traditional rulers were neither involved, nor have they ever been involved in the process of nomination and emergence of any party’s candidate.

“We are further aware that there has not been any record whatsoever from 2019, when the present administration came on board, up till date which indicated that our Royal Fathers and/or State Elders, leaders and the critical stakeholders in Kwara State, were ever mobilised to pay a visit to thank Mr. President for the honour done to prominent sons and daughters of Kwara State, who were appointed into key federal ministries, agencies and parastatals in the past.

“We have also neither heard of an advocacy visit by our Governor to the Presidency, in order to lobby or canvass for opportunities for the State nor learnt of an occasion, where he had deemed it fit to mobilize the citizens of the state to go and thank Mr. President on the efforts being made by the Federal Government to tame the ugly security situation in Kwara State.

“Therefore, we would like to appeal to our dignified and respected Traditional Rulers not to allow any person, however highly placed, to drag them and our Traditional Institution into the affairs of Political Parties or to engage in partisan politics.”
summary: The article reports on a plot by some APC members in Kwara State to force traditional rulers to back the Speaker as the party's governorship candidate in 2027. This means that political instability in Kwara State could potentially impact investor confidence in the Nigerian real estate market, particularly in the mid-term. No direct connection to Mixta's projects in Lagos.
trending_topics: Nigerian politics, Kwara State, APC, governorship election

title: Penzo Homes launches agro-investment, land banking products for Nigerian investors
description: Penzo Homes launches agro-investment, land banking products for Nigerian investors  Vanguard News
content: A Nigerian real estate development company, Penzo Homes & Properties Limited, has unveiled two new investment products aimed at expanding wealth creation opportunities through agriculture and land ownership. The company announced the launch of Penzo Agro City and Penzo LandVest yesterday, describing the initiatives as innovative solutions designed to provide structured and sustainable investment opportunities for Nigerians seeking to build long-term wealth. According to the company, Penzo Agro City is a managed agricultural estate that allows investors to acquire farmland while professional teams handle cultivation, farm management, harvesting, and produce marketing on their behalf. The project is divided into three investment clusters: plantain, oil palm, and cocoa. Penzo Homes noted that the model is designed to enable investors participate in commercial agriculture without requiring direct farming experience or daily operational involvement. The plantain cluster targets a commodity with strong local and export demand, while the palm cluster provides access to one of West Africa’s most profitable perennial crops. The cocoa cluster, it added, focuses on export-oriented agricultural production, with management oversight covering the entire value chain from cultivation to post-harvest processing. Penzo Homes emphasized that transparency, professional farm management, and structured reporting would be central to the operation of the agricultural estate. Alongside the agricultural offering, the company also introduced Penzo LandVest, a land banking initiative aimed at investors seeking long-term capital appreciation through strategic land acquisition. The firm explained that the platform identifies land in growth corridors with verified documentation and development potential, allowing investors to benefit from future increases in land value as urban expansion and infrastructure projects progress. According to the company, the initiative seeks to address common concerns associated with land ownership in Nigeria, including title verification challenges, disputes, and fraudulent transactions. Speaking on the company’s vision, Penzo Homes revealed that it intends to focus on value creation, transparency, and investor confidence rather than competing solely on pricing. The company argued that Nigeria’s growing population, rapid urbanisation, and increasing demand for property investment opportunities have created a need for more structured and reliable real estate solutions. Maintaining that combining agricultural investments with land banking provides investors with diversified opportunities for wealth creation through a single platform. The company noted further that its long-term objective is to develop sustainable communities while creating investment vehicles that support economic growth and financial inclusion across the country. Founded as a real estate development company, Penzo Homes & Properties Limited focuses on residential development, land
summary: Penzo Homes & Properties Limited has launched two new investment products: Penzo Agro City, a managed agricultural estate with three investment clusters (plantain, oil palm, and cocoa), and Penzo LandVest, a land banking initiative. This development indicates growing interest in alternative investment opportunities and land ownership in Lagos, potentially influencing demand for land in growth corridors. The article does not provide specific details on Penzo's projects, but it may impact the broader Lagos real estate market.
trending_topics: agriculture, land ownership, alternative investments, Lagos real estate

title: NDLEA Auctions Lagos Hotel, 3 Other Drug-linked Properties For N6.1bn
description: NDLEA Auctions Lagos Hotel, 3 Other Drug-linked Properties For N6.1bn  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has has auctioned four properties forfeited by convicted drug traffickers to the federal government for a total of N6.149 billion. The auction, conducted through a competitive bidding process at the NDLEA headquarters in Abuja on Monday, saw a six-storey hotel located in Victoria Island, Lagos, attract the highest bid of N5.9 billion. Tope Ojo and Tunde Olonishakin Estate Firm emerged winner of the Victoria Island hotel with a bid of N5.9billion. FSS Limited secured a property located in Lekki Phase 1, Lagos, for N219.5 million, while A-BNB Global Innovations Limited won a block of flats in Ejigbo, Lagos, with a bid of N104 million. Fazeen Global Link Limited acquired a property in Akure, Ondo State, for N29.36 million. Head of the asset recovery and management unit of the Federal Ministry of Justice, Tamarantare Francis Ali-Bozi, announced the successful bidders. Speaking at the bid-opening ceremony, the chairman and chief executive of NDLEA, Brig. Gen. Mohamed Marwa (retd.), said the exercise underscored the federal government’s determination to ensure that criminals do not benefit from proceeds derived from illicit drug trafficking. RELATED NEWS Alleged N110bn Fraud: Court Dismisses Ex-Governor Bello’s Application Challenging Court’s Jurisdiction Rüdiger Commits Future To Real Madrid With One-Year Contract Extension Tinubu Commissions Appeal Court Judges’ Residential Quarters In Abuja Marwa, who was represented by the agency secretary, Shadrach Haruna, stated that public auctions of recovered assets serve a broader national purpose beyond revenue generation. According to him, “Public auctions such as this reinforce public confidence in the rule of law, demonstrate accountability in the management of recovered assets, and send a clear message that criminal proceeds will not be allowed to remain in the hands of those who seek to profit from the misery and destruction caused by illicit drugs.” The NDLEA further stated although eight forfeited assets linked to drug kingpins were listed for sale, however, only four attracted bids above their reserved prices, while bids for the remaining four properties fell below the approved valuation and were consequently unsuccessful. Marwa assured stakeholders that the agency remained committed to strengthening Nigeria’s asset recovery framework and ensuring that recovered assets are managed and disposed of transparently and in accordance with the law. title: Real Estate Sector Battles Liquidity Crisis
description: Real Estate Sector Battles Liquidity Crisis  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: The founder of GText Holdings, Stephen Akintayo, has said that liquidity challenges remain one of the biggest threats confronting operators in Nigeria’s real estate sector. Akintayo disclosed this in Lagos during a news conference held over the weekend as part of activities marking the company’s 18th anniversary. According to him, the structure of real estate investments often ties down capital for long periods, making it difficult for firms to maintain adequate cash flow. He said, “Real estate is structured in a way where capital is tied down for a long time. If you don’t trade and have other businesses you can take on, you will have liquidity problems.” The real estate entrepreneur noted that several operators had faced similar challenges in recent years, stressing that not every struggling company deliberately set out to defraud investors. “We have had our own fair share of liquidity issues. A lot of our colleagues also went through those liquidity challenges,” he said. He added, “Not all real estate companies are trying to play games. Many have liquidity issues and are trying to unlock liquidity in ways that did not work out as planned.” Akintayo said the company was responding to the challenges in the sector through diversification into agriculture, renewable energy, security technology, e-commerce and artificial intelligence-driven businesses. According to him, the firm’s new strategy includes Ginido, a green energy and security solutions venture, and GText Farms, which focuses on agricultural land banking and commercial farming. “We are responding to the challenge through aggressive diversification into agriculture, renewable energy, e-commerce, security technology and artificial intelligence-driven businesses,” he said. He added that the company was also restructuring to attract institutional investors as part of its expansion plans. “We built this company largely without relying on bank loans. Going forward, we are restructuring to begin attracting institutional investors,” Akintayo added. The GText founder further disclosed plans to expand into private security solutions through partnerships with major CCTV manufacturers in China, adding that the company intended to begin assembling security cameras locally before eventually producing them in Nigeria. Speaking on artificial intelligence, Akintayo said the company had achieved over 50 per cent AI adoption among its workforce. “For us, AI is not optional. One person can now do the work of 20 people by leveraging AI,” he said. He, however, warned that the technology could significantly reduce white-collar jobs globally. “I project that about 70 per cent of white-collar jobs will disappear. That is why young people must embrace practical and entrepreneurial skills,” he said. Akintayo said the company had also trained realtors as part of its anniversary activities to improve professionalism in the real estate industry. The four-day programme, he said, focused on digital marketing, land titli
summary: Liquidity constraints highlighted by GText Holdings underscore a systemic cash‑flow challenge for Nigerian developers, raising the risk of project delays and financing squeezes in Lagos. The sector’s capital‑intensive model may dampen new‑launch momentum, especially for lower‑margin housing segments.

title: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’
description: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’ By : Victor Gbonegun Date: 15 June 2026 4:38am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover 7-Fifteen Capital 7-Fifteen Capital A Private developer, 7-Fifteen Capital Limited, has said sustained economic stability and market predictability are critical to the growth of Nigeria’s luxury real estate sector. Speaking at the launch of the company’s Lagos office, the Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder, Tope Adekoya, said foreign exchange volatility and rising inflation had significantly increased construction costs in recent years, posing major challenges for developers. According to him, projects initially valued at $10 million now cost nearly double due to the heavy reliance on imported construction materials. “The luxury real estate market has faced difficult conditions over the past six years because of exchange rate fluctuations and inflation. Developers must adopt creative financing models and focus on building functional, right-sized homes that meet market needs,” he said. Adekoya noted that the outlook for the luxury housing market would largely depend on economic policies and exchange rate stability, which provide developers with greater certainty over project costs. He said the company’s expansion into Lagos follows successful developments in Abuja, including Myari Lakeside, The Residence and The Cove Lakeside, adding that the firm remains committed to delivering affordable luxury housing. Related News Hamzat-Sonayon-James ticket and future of Lagos governance Alimosho APC seeks Tinubu’s intervention over alleged plans to overturn primary result Sanwo-Olu scouts climate investment in London Also speaking, Deputy Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder, Adesuwa Belo-Osagie, said the company intends to contribute to community development through its foundation by supporting education and creating opportunities for young people. Group Chief Executive Officer, Tolu Anyakoya, said the firm’s projects focus on delivering quality living experiences through secure environments and reliable infrastructure, including electricity, water supply and security services. He disclosed that the company currently has about 10 estates under development in Abuja and two projects in Lagos, located in Ikoyi and Banana Island. Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, stressed the importance of public-private partnerships in building world-class cities, noting that no government can achieve sustainable urban development alone. He said the private sector contributes capital, technology and speed, while the government provides the planning framework and enabling environment required for growth. Sanwo-Olu urged developers and investors to focus on projects that create lasting value for future generations, describing Lagos as a city of boundless opportunities driven by collaboration and innovation. Tags: 7-Fifteen Capital Limited Babajide Sanwo-Olu
summary: Economic stability is crucial for the growth of Nigeria's luxury real estate sector, posing significant challenges for developers due to foreign exchange volatility and rising inflation, which may impact Mixta Africa's premium and commercial projects.

title: Lagos commits to affordable mortgage scheme, certifies homeowners
description: Lagos commits to affordable mortgage scheme, certifies homeowners  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Lagos commits to affordable mortgage scheme, certifies homeowners By : Guardian Nigeria Date: 15 June 2026 4:22am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover LagosHOMS LagosHOMS By Godwin Ofulue The Lagos State Mortgage Board (LMB) has reaffirmed the government’s commitment to expanding affordable housing opportunities for residents through its Home Ownership Mortgage Scheme (LagosHOMS). Speaking at the Fourth Exit Ceremony for beneficiaries of LagosHOMS, the General Manager and Chief Executive Officer of the board, Mr Bayowa Foresythe, said the programme was designed to bridge the housing gap by providing affordable and sustainable homeownership opportunities, particularly for low-income earners. Foresythe said more than 30,000 residents had benefited from the mortgage initiative, also known as the Rent-To-Own Scheme, since its inception. According to him, beneficiaries cut across various age groups, professions, genders and socio-economic backgrounds, including civil servants, artisans, entrepreneurs, market women and workers in both the formal and informal sectors. “This demonstrates that LagosHOMS is a truly inclusive programme. Allocation of homes is based on eligibility criteria and affordability assessments, ensuring transparency, fairness and equal opportunity for all qualified applicants,” he said. Related News APC chieftain dismisses claims linking Yari to alleged EFCC gold seizure Kwara APC stakeholders back Danladi’s candidacy, defend governorship primary outcome Group urges Kwara APC to heed elders’ concerns over governorship primary He explained that the ceremony marked the successful completion of mortgage tenures by beneficiaries, some of whom also took advantage of the scheme’s redemption policy to complete payments ahead of schedule. Congratulating the beneficiaries, Foresythe praised their discipline, perseverance and commitment despite prevailing economic challenges. “Today, your resilience has paid off. You are no longer mortgagors or tenants; you are now proud homeowners in Lagos State,” he said. He attributed the success of the scheme to housing reforms initiated during the administration of President Bola Tinubu as Governor of Lagos State and commended Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu and Deputy Governor Dr Kadri Hamzat for their continued support. Foresythe also acknowledged the contributions of strategic partners, including the Lagos Building Investment Company (LBIC), LASACO Assurance Plc, and First City Monument Bank (FCMB), in advancing the state’s affordable housing objectives. Some beneficiaries described the scheme as accessible and affordable, citing its flexible repayment structure and single-digit interest rate as key advantages. Tags: Affordable mortgage scheme Bola Tinubu LagosHoms LASACO LBIC LMB

title: FG expands access to affordable housing in Kano
description: FG expands access to affordable housing in Kano  Peoples Gazette Nigeria
content: FG expands access to affordable housing in Kano According to Mr Garba, Nigerians can now access loans to purchase homes and repay over several years based on mutually agreed terms. News Agency of Nigeria • June 14, 2026 newly-built houses The federal government has expanded access to affordable home ownership through its Renewed Hope housing scheme, making it easier for Nigerians to own homes with flexible mortgage options. Malam Ahmad Garba, branch manager at the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria in Kano, disclosed this during a stakeholder engagement organised by QShelter Limited, a private-sector partner delivering affordable housing under the initiative. According to Mr Garba, Nigerians can now access loans to purchase homes and repay over several years based on mutually agreed terms. “The process is designed to be seamless. Once the conditions are signed, buyers can settle payments within a reasonable timeframe,” he said. Speaking at the event, Dare Makinde, chief commercial officer of QShelter Limited, said the firm had come from Lagos to engage investors, agents, and other stakeholders in the housing ecosystem. “We want to showcase what we have accomplished in Kano under the Renewed Hope housing partnership. “395 housing units have been completed at Renewed Hope City, Janguza, Kano, and are awaiting inauguration by President Bola Tinubu, after which keys will be handed over to owners,” Mr Makinde stated. He argued that, contrary to public assumptions about high costs, the houses were both affordable and accessible. He noted that the mortgage process had been simplified, saying that buyers pay only 10 per cent of the property price, while the mortgage bank covers the balance for those opting for the loan scheme. In her remarks, Bilkisu Ibrahim, director of Architecture at the Kano State Urban Planning and Development Authority (KNUPDA), said the scheme aligned with the Kano State government’s agenda to provide affordable housing for low-income earners. “With this development, we hope to see a newly developed Kano state within the next five years,” she said. title: Jim Ovia swells rank of unlikely entrants into real estate
description: Jim Ovia swells rank of unlikely entrants into real estate  Business News Nigeria
content: Share Jim Ovia, the billionaire founder of Nigeria’s largest bank by market value, has made an unexpected full-time pivot to real estate, betting that luxury residential real estate in Lagos will yield higher returns than commercial lending. Ovia, who concluded a 12-year tenure as chairman of Zenith Bank Plc on May 5, is deploying capital into the premium residential sector through Quantum Luxury Properties Ltd. His much-talked-about project, the 26-floor Metropolitan Towers on the Lagos Lagoon, features unit prices starting at $1.85 million. A sister development, the 44-unit Quantum Luxury Towers, prices apartments from $2.8 million, targeting high-net-worth individuals (HNIs). Both developments, which are selling fast according to Ovia, are located strategically where he constructed Civic Towers and Civic Centre, where events are held. Ovia is part of a broader trend of non-traditional developers aggressively building across commercial and residential hubs in Nigeria’s commercial capital. Tony Elumelu has been in the market for a long time with his Afriland Properties, which has left strong footprints in that market. Afriland Properties is a Property Development Company located on Broad Street, Lagos Island, Lagos. The company has two ongoing projects, eight completed projects and five high-profile key contacts. Similarly, Matthew Ashimolowo, the Senior Pastor of the Kingsway International Christian Centre (KICC), is a prominent Nigerian real estate developer, investor, and the Chairman of Makarios Luxury Properties Limited and Meridian Luxury Properties Limited. He is well known for developing massive luxury and destination estates in the fast-growing Ibeju-Lekki corridor of Lagos, Nigeria. Read also: Nigerian tycoon-backed financier eyes East Africa expansion in bold pan-African growth push One of these flagship projects is Makarios the Luxury Place—an 84-acre multimillion-dollar development along the Lekki-Epe Expressway featuring revolutionary amenities, residential plots, and commercial hubs. Another is Makarios Champions Place – a sports-themed estate designed specifically for football fans, where streets and sections are named after major European football clubs like Chelsea, Arsenal, and Manchester United. Though it is not primarily a commercial real estate developer, the Redeemed Christian Church of God (RCCG), especially its larger, autonomous parishes, frequently undertakes massive real estate development and infrastructural projects to fund charitable works and accommodate its vast congregations. An example of such projects is Trinity Towers, built in Oniru, Victoria Island, Lagos, by the RCCG City of David parish. The 14-storey tri-tower commercial edifice was specifically designed to generate rental income to fund their extensive Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) and charitable programmes. Ovia may have left Zenith Bank; however, the billionaire remains the lender’s largest individual shareholder, suggesting that he is far fro
summary: Jim Ovia's pivot to luxury residential real estate in Lagos signals a shift in investor sentiment, potentially driving up demand for premium properties and pressuring Mixta Africa to adapt its sales mix towards higher-margin segments.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This development may pressure Mixta Africa to shift its new-sales mix towards higher-margin segments, such as premium Lakowe Lakes products and LNT commercial plots.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Jim Ovia's entry into the luxury residential market may drive up prices and costs for Mixta Africa, potentially affecting its pricing and profitability.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Mixta Africa can capitalize on the trend by targeting high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) and adapting its product offerings to meet the growing demand for luxury properties.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The increased competition from non-traditional developers like Jim Ovia may threaten Mixta Africa's market share and execution of its strategic priorities.

title: Anambra Plans Mass Housing Policy
description: Anambra Plans Mass Housing Policy  Voice of Nigeria
content: Anambra Plans Mass Housing Policy  Voice of Nigeria
summary: Anambra's mass housing policy may increase competition for affordable housing in Lagos, potentially pressuring Mixta Africa's pricing and sales mix, but also presents opportunities for institutional transactions and partnerships.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The policy may increase competition for affordable housing in Lagos, potentially pressuring Mixta Africa's pricing and sales mix, particularly for Lakowe Lakes products.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The policy may also affect the regulatory environment and costs associated with affordable housing development in Lagos.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Mixta Africa may explore partnerships or institutional transactions to capitalize on the increased demand for affordable housing in Lagos.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The policy may pose a risk to Mixta Africa's execution of its strategic priorities, particularly if it leads to increased competition and pricing pressure.
